items.author: fakechris

items.title: google-generative-ai tagged reasoning mode breaks tool calling — models write tool calls as text instead of invoking them
items.content:
### Version
OpenClaw 2026.4.12

### Description

\`provider-utils-COBMH4gY.js\` hardcodes \`google-generative-ai\` as \`"tagged"\` reasoning mode:

\`\`\`js
const BUILTIN_REASONING_OUTPUT_MODES = { "google-generative-ai": "tagged" };
\`\`\`

This causes the following system prompt to be injected on every turn for any Google model (Gemma 4 26B, Gemini Flash, etc.):

> "Format every reply as \`<think>...</think>\` then \`<final>...</final>\`, with no other text. Only the final user-visible reply may appear inside \`<final>\`. Only text inside \`<final>\` is shown to the user; **everything else is discarded and never seen by the user.**"

The bold phrase is the bug. Models correctly follow this instruction — and conclude that tool calls (which are emitted outside the text stream) would be "discarded." So they describe tool calls as prose text inside \`<final>\` instead of actually invoking them. The runtime sees no tool call block, the agent stays idle.

Both Gemma 4 26B and Gemini 2.5 Flash reproduce this. Once \`<think>/<final>\` responses appear in conversation history, even non-Google models (e.g. DeepSeek Chat) mimic the pattern in subsequent turns.

### Reproduction

1. Configure \`google/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it\` as the primary model
2. Ask the agent to call any registered tool: \`"use tool_search to find ata_generate"\`
3. Observe: agent writes \`<tool_call:tool_search(query="ata_generate")>\` as literal text in \`<final>\`, then goes idle — no tool execution occurs

### Expected behavior

Tool calls are a separate mechanism from the text response stream. The \`<think>/<final>\` format instruction should not interfere with tool invocation. Models should be able to produce a \`<final>\` text block AND invoke tool calls in the same turn.

### Workaround

Removing \`"google-generative-ai"\` from \`BUILTIN_REASONING_OUTPUT_MODES\` in the dist restores correct tool calling behavior. Google's native API already handles reasoning — the tagged format is redundant for \`reas

items.title: fix(openrouter): handle reasoning_details in Qwen3 stream parsing
items.content:
### Summary

Fix OpenRouter/Qwen3 models returning empty responses due to unrecognized `reasoning_details` field.

**Problem**: When using `openrouter/qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b` or any Qwen3 model via OpenRouter, every response fails with "incomplete turn detected: payloads=0" and the user sees "⚠️ Agent couldn't generate a response."

**Root Cause**: The stream parser in `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` handled `reasoning_content`, `reasoning`, and `reasoning_text` but not `reasoning_details` — which Qwen3 returns via OpenRouter. The unhandled field caused zero content blocks to be assembled, triggering the incomplete turn path.

**Solution**: Add handling for `reasoning_details` array. Extract text from items with `type: "reasoning.text"` and treat as thinking content, enabling successful turn completion.

**Additional defensive fixes**:
- Add `?? ""` guards in `src/channels/plugins/setup-wizard.ts:458` and `src/channels/plugins/setup-wizard-helpers.ts:994` to prevent potential undefined trim errors from clack wrapper edge cases (belt-and-suspenders approach mentioned in issue comments).

### Changes

- **openai-transport-stream.ts**: Add `reasoning_details` handling in `processOpenAICompletionsStream`, extracting reasoning text from array items and creating thinking blocks.
- **openai-transport-stream.test.ts**: Add test case verifying correct handling of Qwen3 reasoning_details.
- **setup-wizard.ts & setup-wizard-helpers.ts**: Add defensive guards around prompter.text() results.

### Test plan

- [x] New unit test: `handles reasoning_details from OpenRouter/Qwen3 in completions stream` passes
- [x] All 53 existing `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` tests pass
- [x] Fix verified by community members in original issue thread

### AI Code Generation Disclosure

- 🤖 This PR was created with AI assistance (Claude Codex)
- The fix implementation and test were generated based on issue description and community diagnosis

### Environment

- OpenClaw version: 2026.4.14

items.title: feat: Confusion Protocol, Hermes + GBrain hosts, brain-first resolver (v0.18.0.0)
items.content:
## Summary

**Agent runtime support + Karpathy-inspired guardrails + skill improvements.**

**Confusion Protocol** — inline ambiguity gate in the preamble. When Claude hits a
decision that could go two ways (which architecture? which data model? destructive
operation with unclear scope?), it stops and asks instead of guessing. Scoped to
high-stakes decisions only. Addresses Karpathy failure mode #1 (wrong assumptions).

**Hermes + GBrain host configs** — two new hosts. Hermes gets tool rewrites for
`terminal`/`read_file`/`patch`/`delegate_task`. GBrain is a "mod" for gstack:
coding skills become brain-aware when installed, searching the brain for context
before starting and saving results after finishing.

**GBrain resolver** — `GBRAIN_CONTEXT_LOAD` and `GBRAIN_SAVE_RESULTS` injected into
4 thinking skill templates (office-hours, investigate, ceo-review, retro). Suppressed
on all 9 non-gbrain hosts. For gbrain host, skills get brain-first lookup and
save-to-brain behavior.

**slop:diff in /review** — every code review now runs `bun run slop:diff` as advisory
diagnostic, catching AI code quality issues before they land.

**Karpathy compatibility** — README positions gstack as the workflow enforcement layer
for Karpathy-style CLAUDE.md rules (17K stars).

**Skill improvements** — CEO review HARD GATE at 12 STOP points, office-hours design
doc path visibility, investigate investigation learnings, retro non-git context.
Native OpenClaw skills mirrored.

**Infrastructure** — host count 8→10, GBRAIN suppression on all hosts, dead code
cleanup (openclaw adapter removal), golden fixture updates.

## Test Coverage

737 tests pass, 0 failures. Changes are markdown templates + TypeScript configs.
No new application codepaths — coverage audit: N/A (template/config changes).

## Pre-Landing Review

No issues found. All changes are TypeScript host configs, markdown templates,
resolver functions, and documentation.

Claude subagent: 6 findings (setup auto-de

items.title: 📰 科研 & 技术热点日报 - 2026-04-15 上午
items.content:
# 🧠 科研 & 技术热点日报

日期：2026-04-15 上午
生成时间：2026/04/15 08:34

## 📝 今日总结

# 2026年4月15日 科研与技术热点新闻总结

## 1. 今日最重要的技术趋势和热点

**（1）AI Agent 从“能做”到“做好”：评估体系与安全能力同步演进**  
今日新闻的核心焦点是 **AI Agent 能力的深度评估与安全实践**。  
- **评估体系升级**：LABBench2 的提出标志着 AI 在科研领域（特别是生物学）的评估，正从“知识问答”转向“真实工作能力”的衡量。这呼应了 AI 系统从“辅助工具”向“自主研究主体”演进的趋势。
- **安全成为显学**：GitHub 发布的 **“Secure Code Game”** 和 OpenAI 的 **“Trusted Access for Cyber”** 项目（推出 GPT-5.4-Cyber）形成鲜明对比：一方是让开发者学习攻击和防御 AI Agent，另一方是向专业防御者提供先进 AI 工具。这揭示了产业界对 AI 安全的两手准备：**普及安全意识**与**构建专业壁垒**。

**（2）大语言模型的“自省”与“对齐”进入新阶段**  
- **自校准（Self-Calibration）**：论文《Self-Calibrating Language Models via Test-Time Discriminative Distillation》提出了让 LLM 在推理时进行自我判别式蒸馏，以解决其“系统性过度自信”问题。这本质上是让模型学会说“我不知道”，是提升可靠性的关键一步。
- **规模化对齐（Scalable Oversight）**：Anthropic 的研究“Automated Alignment Researchers”探讨用 LLM 来规模化进行 AI 对齐研究，这指向了解决超级对齐（Superalignment）问题的一种可能路径：**用 AI 来监督和提升 AI 自身的安全性**。

**（3）工程化落地：从 RAG 到“上下文层”，从 CI 到“上下文感知”**  
- **RAG 的反思与进化**：有观点直言“RAG Isn’t Enough”，并提出了构建“缺失的上下文层”。这表明业界已不满足于简单的检索-生成模式，开始追求更深度的知识整合与情境理解系统。
- **软件工程的智能化与理性化**：关于持续集成（CI）的论文提出 **“Context-Aware CI Adoption Decisions”** ，反对盲目采用 CI，主张根据项目上下文理性决策。同时，Zup 公司构建内部编码代理（CodeGen）的经验分享表明，**工具设计、安全护栏和状态管理**等工程决策，其重要性不亚于模型本身的质量。

## 2. 值得关注的研究方向或突破

- **基础理论连接**：《The Diffusion-Attention Connection》论文试图统一 Transformer（注意力）、扩散模型和磁拉普拉斯算子，揭示它们都源于预 softmax 查询分数的马尔可夫几何。这种**底层数学框架的统一探索**可能为未来新模型架构带来灵感。
- **公平性评估框架**：《Fairboard》研究对18个开源脑瘤分割模型进行了多维公平性评估，发现**患者身份特征（如诊断、肿瘤分级）对模型性能差异的解释力，强于模型架构本身的选择**。这为医疗AI的公平性研究提供了严谨的量化框架和重要洞见：数据与临床背景可能比算法更重要。
- **推理加速的标准化评估**：SPEED-Bench 作为首个统一的推测解码（Speculative Decoding）基准，旨在解决该技术评估中任务单一、脱离生产环境的问题。这反映了**LLM推理加速技术从“可用”到“可可靠评估”** 的成熟化进程。
- **并行计算突破**：Para-B&B 在 HiGHS 求解器中实现了首个开源的、确定性的并行混合整数规划（MIP）分支定界算法，解决了该领域长期存在的并行异构性挑战，对运筹优化领域有实质推动。

## 3. 简要分析与展望

今日新闻透露出一个清晰的信号：**AI 技术，特别是 Agent 和 LLM，正在经历从“能力演示”到“生产就绪”的深水区转型**。评估标准转向真实任务效能，安全从附加项变成核心工程，工程实践强调上下文感知与理性采纳。

可以预见：
1.  **专业化、垂直化的 AI Agent 评估基准**将大量涌现，成为衡量领域智能体的“标尺”。
2.  **AI 安全生态将两极分化**：一方面是开源社区推动的攻防演练和安全意识普及，另一方面是巨头构建的、面向专业领域的受信任工具和平台。
3.  **L

# AI Ecosystem Radar — Wide View (2026-04-15)

The AI ecosystem is currently undergoing a high-friction **"Stability vs. Autonomy"** transition. gstack 的 plan-design-review 引入 Krug + Redish + Jarrett 参考体系，说明设计原则的积累对 AI 决策质量至关重要。但当前 AI Coding 缺乏持久化的设计记忆，每次上下文都是空的。

items.title: WIP: feat(memory): typed memory index with composable provider interface
items.content:
## Summary

- Adds a composable `MemoryProvider` interface for NemoClaw's memory system, with `TypedMemoryProvider` as the first implementation
- Transforms MEMORY.md from a flat file into a lightweight index table, with full content in individual topic files under `memory/topics/`
- **~59% context window token savings** at 1,000+ entries (measured with tiktoken inside sandbox container)
- Agent-callable tools (`nemoclaw_memory_save`, `nemoclaw_memory_read`, `nemoclaw_memory_search`) registered via OpenClaw plugin API
- Enable/disable toggle: `/nemoclaw memory enable` and `/nemoclaw memory disable` -- disabled by default, user opts in
- Container-based benchmark suite (`npm run benchmark:memory`) with full methodology and report

### Key design decisions

- **Composable**: Defines a `MemoryProvider` interface that OpenClaw can adopt for their composable memory system. NemoClaw proposes the contract, ships one implementation.
- **Opt-in**: Typed memory is disabled by default. The agent uses OpenClaw's standard flat MEMORY.md until the user runs `/nemoclaw memory enable`.
- **Honest tradeoffs**: Below ~10 entries, flat is smaller. Tool call overhead adds tokens per read. Benchmark shows the full picture including crossover point.
- **Type alignment**: Uses OpenClaw's memory types (`user | project | feedback | reference`).

### Files changed (24 files, ~3,700 lines)

| Area | Files | What |
|---|---|---|
| Provider interface | `memory/provider.ts` | `MemoryProvider` interface |
| Implementation | `memory/typed-provider.ts` | `TypedMemoryProvider` with CRUD, search, migrate |
| Config + AGENTS.md | `memory/config.ts` | Enable/disable toggle, AGENTS.md injection |
| Agent tools | `memory/tools.ts` | `nemoclaw_memory_save/read/search` tools |
| Slash commands | `commands/slash.ts` | read, search, list, migrate, enable, disable |
| Plugin wiring | `index.ts` | Tool registration via `api.registerTool()` |
| Docs | `workspace-files.md` | Updated workspace file reference |

items.title: feat(bluebubbles): replay missed webhook messages after gateway restart (#66721)
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes #66721. Adds an in-process startup catchup pass to the BlueBubbles channel that queries BB Server for messages delivered while the gateway was unreachable and replays them through the existing `processMessage` pipeline.

**The hole this closes:** BB Server's `WebhookService` is fire-and-forget on POST failure (no retries) and BB's `MessagePoller` only re-fires webhooks on BB-side reconnection events (Messages.app / APNs), not on webhook-receiver recovery. Messages delivered while the gateway was down, restarting, or wedged were permanently lost — verified with a controlled experiment on macOS.

This PR supersedes #66853 (which was the stacked follow-up to #66760 / dedupe PR #66816). Same diff, collapsed to a single commit for cleaner review. History of review feedback is preserved in the superseded PR trail; all P1 and P2 findings from Greptile / Codex / Aisle were addressed in-branch before this squash.

## Design

- **New `extensions/bluebubbles/src/catchup.ts`:**
  - `fetchBlueBubblesMessagesSince(sinceMs, limit, opts)` calls `/api/v1/message/query` with `{after, sort:"ASC", with:["chat","chat.participants","attachment"]}` so replays carry the same shape `normalizeWebhookMessage` already handles on live dispatch.
  - `loadBlueBubblesCatchupCursor` / `saveBlueBubblesCatchupCursor` persist a single `{lastSeenMs, updatedAt}` per account under `<stateDir>/bluebubbles/catchup/<accountId>__<hash>.json`, using the plugin-sdk's atomic JSON helpers. File layout mirrors the inbound-dedupe store from #66816, and the resolver is the canonical `openclaw/plugin-sdk/state-paths.resolveStateDir` (same helper dedupe uses) so the two stores share a single root.
  - `runBlueBubblesCatchup(target)` orchestrates: clamp config, fetch, filter `isFromMe` and pre-cursor records, dispatch to `processMessage`, advance cursor.
- **Modified `monitor.ts`:** fire catchup as a background task after webhook target registers; errors are logged but never block the channel-ready s

items.title: fix(skills): prompts inside [skill] TOML section were silently dropped
items.content:
> **Python Skills Enablement — PR 3 of 5**
> This is part of a series fixing bugs that prevent **any** Python-based skill from working on ZeroClaw. PRs 1–4 are independent bug fixes; PR 5 adds a new tool. Together they bring ZeroClaw to parity with OpenClaw for Python skill execution.
> 
> | # | PR | What it unblocks |
> |---|-----|-----------------|
> | 1 | #5747 | `allow_scripts = true` config is actually honoured |
> | 2 | #5748 | `python3` allowed on Pi; Docker sandbox gets workspace mount |
> | **3** | **#5749 (this)** | **`[skill].prompts` are injected into model context** |
> | 4 | #5750 | `runtime.kind = "native"` skips Docker auto-selection |
> | 5 | #5751 | `model_spawn` tool (live switch + parallel ephemeral spawns) |

## What this unblocks

**Without this fix**: any skill that puts `prompts` inside `[skill]` (the intuitive, documented location) gets zero prompt injection — `prompt_injection_mode = "full"` appears to have no effect. This affects every TOML-based skill on ZeroClaw, not just InvestorClaw.

**With this fix**: both `[skill].prompts` (canonical) and top-level `prompts` (legacy) work. Existing skills are unaffected; new skills can use the intuitive form.

---

## Problem

When a skill manifest placed `prompts` inside the `[skill]` table (the natural location), they were silently discarded. `SkillMeta` had no `prompts` field, so TOML deserialization threw them away before `skills_to_prompt_with_mode` ever saw them.

As a result, `prompt_injection_mode = "full"` never injected skill instructions into the model system prompt, regardless of config. The mode appeared to have no effect.

**Root cause in `src/skills/mod.rs`:**

```toml
# user writes this — intuitive location:
[skill]
name        = "investorclaw"
description = "Portfolio analysis"
prompts     = ["You are a portfolio analyst ..."]  # ← silently dropped

# only this form was actually read:
prompts = ["..."]   # top-level — non-obvious
```

## Fix

- Add `prompts: Vec<String>` to `SkillMe
items.author: perlowjanv

items.title: feat(tools): per-sender tool capability tiers via toolsBySender
items.content:
## Summary

- Adds `tools.toolsBySender` (global) and `agents.<id>.tools.toolsBySender` (per-agent override) config fields that strip tools from the LLM's schema on a per-sender basis — denied capabilities are genuinely invisible to the model for that user, not just blocked at runtime
- Adds platform-scoped compound key format (`discord:<snowflake>`, `telegram:<userId>`, etc.) to prevent cross-platform sender ID collisions — works in both the new global policy and existing group-level `toolsBySender` configs
- Threads `messageProvider` through `GroupToolPolicySender` so all `toolsBySender` lookups are platform-aware

## Motivation

Right now there's no way to give different users different capability levels. If a friend messages your OpenClaw instance and asks it to run a destructive shell command, OpenClaw will attempt it. The owner should have full access; guests should get a restricted toolset baked into the model's context at the schema level so the LLM doesn't even know those tools exist for them.

## Key formats

| Key | Matches |
|-----|---------|
| `discord:123456789012345678` | That Discord snowflake only |
| `telegram:987654321` | That Telegram user ID only |
| `<channel>:<senderId>` | Generic platform-scoped form |
| `id:<senderId>` | Raw sender ID (all platforms) |
| `e164:+15551234567` | Phone number |
| `username:bob` | Platform username |
| `*` | Wildcard fallback |

Match priority: channel-scoped → id → e164 → username → name → `*`

## Example `openclaw.json`

```json
{
  "tools": {
    "toolsBySender": {
      "discord:123456789012345678": {},
      "telegram:987654321": {},
      "*": { "deny": ["exec", "process", "write", "edit"] }
    }
  }
}
```

Run `/whoami` in any channel to get your senderId and channel name.

## Files changed

- `src/config/types.tools.ts` — `toolsBySender` field on `ToolsConfig` and `AgentToolsConfig`
- `src/config/group-policy.ts` — `messageProvider` on `GroupToolPolicySender`, `channel` bucket, compound key detection
- `

items.title: tool dispatcher regression
items.content:
#35 Thank you for your cooperation.

This fix resolved the “Read” issue, but ‘Glob’ and “Bash” still do not work properly in OpenClaw.

---

## Bash tool — always Unknown action <command>

```
input:  { "command": "pwd" }
output: "Unknown action pwd"
```

Looks like the Bash handler is reading payload.action instead of payload.command, so it treats the command string as an unknown action. Possibly confused with the process tool (which is action-based).

## Glob tool — mis-routed to image

```
input:  { "pattern": "/tmp/*" }
output: { "status":"error", "tool":"image", "error":"image required" }
```

Glob calls land on the image handler, which then fails its own required-param check. Smells like a tool-registry binding/alias mistake.

## Minimal repro
Bash: { "command": "pwd" } → Unknown action pwd
Glob: { "pattern": "/tmp/*" } → {"tool":"image","error":"image required"}

**Environment:**
- OS: macOS 26.4.1
- Node version: v25.9.0
- dario version: 3.9.3
- Tool using dario (e.g. OpenClaw, Aider): OpenClaw
items.author: tetsuco

items.title: [Release] openclaw 2026.4.14
items.content:
OpenClaw `2026.4.14` is another broad quality release focused on model provider with explicit turn improvements for GPT-5 family and channel provider issues. Additionally we improved overal performance with refactors to our underlying core codebase.

## Changes

- OpenAI Codex/models: add forward-compat support for `gpt-5.4-pro`, including Codex pricing/limits and list/status visibility before the upstream catalog catches up. (#66453) Thanks @jepson-liu.
- Telegram/forum topics: surface human topic names in agent context, prompt metadata, and plugin hook metadata by learning names from Telegram forum service messages. (#65973) Thanks @ptahdunbar.

## Fixes

- Agents/Ollama: forward the configured embedded-run timeout into the global undici stream timeout tuning so slow local Ollama runs no longer inherit the default stream cutoff instead of the operator-set run timeout. (#63175) Thanks @mindcraftreader and @vincentkoc.
- Models/Codex: include `apiKey` in the codex provider catalog output so the Pi ModelRegistry validator no longer rejects the entry and silently drops all custom models from every provider in `models.json`. (#66180) Thanks @hoyyeva.
- Tools/image+pdf: normalize configured provider/model refs before media-tool registry lookup so image and PDF tool runs stop rejecting valid Ollama vision models as unknown just because the tool path skipped the usual model-ref normalization step. (#59943) Thanks @yqli2420 and @vincentkoc.
- Slack/interactions: apply the configured global `allowFrom` owner allowlist to channel block-action and modal interactive events, require an expected sender id for cross-verification, and reject ambiguous channel types so interactive triggers can no longer bypass the documented allowlist intent in channels without a `users` list. Open-by-default behavior is preserved when no allowlists are configured. (#66028) Thanks @eleqtrizit.
- Media-understanding/attachments: fail closed when a local attachment path cannot be canonic
items.author: vincentkoc
